calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lon Lazar for and with (administrator) Lily Grove. Alan Jarumay in Propria Persona. Maryann Pena in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Mr. Lazar if an Evidentiary Hearing is needed. Mr. Lazar stated that the case can be decided without one, that the submissions are sufficient but that he can address an concerns that the Court has. Mr. Jarumay confirmed that they have stated everything that they wanted to state. The Court asked about the attorney's fees listed as distributions to Lily & Gary. Mr. Lazar stated that they were distributions to Lily & Gary, that the value has not diminished and that none of the beneficiaries have suffered detriment. Mr. Jarumay objected, stating that it was not part of the estate, that it referenced the Special Needs Trust and that the payments came out of Gary's trust. Mr. Lazar stated that it is purely fiction, that the fees were paid for services unrelated to the estate and are accounted for. Ms. Pena stated that it is related to the estate, that it was written from the estate and that the memo indicates trust. The Court took the Petition for Final Distribution under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-21-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lon Lazar for and with (administrator) Lily Grove. Alan Jarumay (objector) in Propria Persona. Maryann Flores (objector) also present.

The Court stated that counsel filed a declaration regarding the original accounting statements, that they need to be reviewed and confirmed that Alan is objecting to the fees and other issues. Mr. Jarumay stated that he filed an amendment last week, that it was served and that it has to do with a bank account. Mr. Lazar stated that he received an electronic statement, that it was unclear and that he thought it was a restatement of the objection. Mr. Jarumay confirmed that he did not provide courtesy copies, that he and his sisters want the amendment reviewed and that it had been understood that Ms. Grove would not charge fees. Mr. Lazar stated that the handling of this case should have been simple, that circumstances and communication between the parties changed, that the administrator had to do more work than anticipated and that he told her that she is entitled to the statutory fee. Mr. Jarumay stated that they are basing the fees on additional work but do not say what it was, that it has nothing to do with him and his siblings, that Ms. Grove did not explain anything and was not transparent and that they started asking what was going on but did not make more work for her. Ms. Grove stated that she cared for her father, that she sold the home without using a realtor which saved $87,000.00 in fees, that she made repairs, did the staging and sold the property over market value. The Court stated that Ms. Grove is entitled to fees unless it was waived in writing. The Court stated that it would take the issue of fees under submission and continue the objection of further issues to December 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Subsequent to the hearing the Court decided that the entire matter would be continued to the above date and the clerk will notify the parties.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)
